mechanism_narrative: A 5-month growth acceleration in the US GDP signals an expansion in aggregate demand that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth acceleration fails to predict positive forward returns in > 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the industrial production index signals a supply-side shock that reduces corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production decline fails to predict negative forward returns in > 60% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a monetary tightening cycle that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Federal funds rate rise fails to predict negative forward returns in > 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4% signals a labor market tightening that boosts wage growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Unemployment rate decline fails to predict positive forward returns in > 60% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the housing starts index above 1.2 million signals a demand-side shock that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts rise fails to predict positive forward returns in > 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the industrial production index and a 2-month rise in the unemployment rate signal a supply-side shock and labor market tightening that reduce corporate earnings and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: supply-side shock and labor market tightening
failure_condition: Combined signal fails to predict negative forward returns in > 60% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% and a 3-month decline in the industrial production index signal a monetary tightening cycle and supply-side shock that compress equity valuations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle and supply-side shock
failure_condition: Combined signal fails to predict negative forward returns in > 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month growth acceleration in the US GDP and a 2-month rise in the housing starts index signal an expansion in aggregate demand that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined signal fails to predict positive forward returns in > 60% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.
